items.prompt: classifies studies, evaluates samples, and makes bounded informal inferences from sample data
items.expected_evidence: make and defend bounded population inferences from sample summaries while naming uncertainty
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner aligns the inference claim with the sample and study-design structure.

items.prompt: explains how random sampling, bias, variability, and study design determine what can be inferred from a sample
items.expected_evidence: explain how sample structure affects what can be inferred about a larger population
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains inference as conditional on design quality and variability, not as automatic generalization.

items.prompt: Diagnose a peer error aligned to causal_claim_leap.
items.expected_evidence: classify bounded studies as survey, observational study, or experiment and state the claim limits
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies where a study-design limit was ignored and an unsupported claim was made.

items.prompt: interprets a less-familiar study or sample summary and checks whether the reported inference is actually supported
items.expected_evidence: identify sources of sampling bias, confounding, or measurement weakness in bounded studies
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves design meaning and identifies the main threat to the reported conclusion.
